title: The thought is not the thinker.
body: Your mind produces thoughts the way your heart produces beats. You're the one watching them, not the one whose word they are. Treating every thought as a verdict is the source of most needless suffering.
reflectionQuestion: What thought have you been treating as your identity that's actually just a thought?

title: Once you can name a distortion, half its power is gone.
body: All-or-nothing, mind-reading, catastrophising, should-statements — each one is a thinking pattern with a name. The naming creates distance. The distance creates choice.
reflectionQuestion: Which distortion have you been doing this week without naming it?

title: All-or-nothing thinking misses 90% of life.
body: Things are not great or ruined, you are not brilliant or stupid, the relationship is not perfect or over. Reality lives in the middle terrain. Black-and-white framing always involves losing information.
reflectionQuestion: Where have you been all-or-nothing about something that's actually 60/40?

title: You're not as good at mind-reading as you think.
body: "They didn't text back because they're mad" is fiction with a confident voice. You don't know. The cost of acting on imagined hostility is high. The cost of asking is low.
reflectionQuestion: Whose state have you decided you know without checking?

title: Catastrophising is fear pretending to be planning.
body: Real risk assessment looks at probability and severity and consequence. Catastrophising just runs the worst-case loop without ever solving for it. The first is useful; the second is exhausting.
reflectionQuestion: Are you assessing risk or running the loop?

title: "Should" is a smuggled-in commandment.
body: Every "I should be more X" carries an unexamined source — your parents, a teacher, an advert, an old version of you. Asking "says who?" is the small move that unhooks the should.
reflectionQuestion: Whose voice is in your most recurring should?

title: "I am X" is just a sentence. Add a verb.
body: "I'm not a runner" becomes "I haven't run in years." "I'm bad with money" becomes "I haven't tracked my spending." The verb version describes behaviour that can change. The identity version pretends to be permanent.
reflectionQuestion: What identity statement could you rewrite as a behaviour sentence?

title: The question you avoid is usually the right one.
body: The questions that make you flinch — am I happy in this job, do I still love this person, why am I drinking that much — are usually the questions whose answers would actually change something. The avoidance is the signal.
reflectionQuestion: What's a question you've been not asking yourself for months?

title: Get the thought out of your head and onto paper.
body: Thoughts move differently in writing. The same loop that ran for an hour in your head takes ten minutes on the page — and after ten minutes you've usually seen it from a new angle. Cheap tool. Huge effect.
reflectionQuestion: What thought has been spinning that you've never actually written down?

title: You can grow. Skip the bumper sticker.
body: Growth mindset isn't "you can do anything if you believe." It's: skills are learnable, effort matters, the current state is not the permanent state. The honest version is less inspiring and more useful.
reflectionQuestion: Where are you treating a current limit as a permanent one?

title: Your story about your life is one of many possible stories.
body: The same events can be told as failure or as growth, as betrayal or as separation, as loss or as freedom. The telling is not the events; the telling is the choosing.
reflectionQuestion: Whose version of your life have you been telling that isn't actually yours?

title: Thinking about a thing is not the same as doing the thing.
body: Months of mental rehearsal feel like progress and are not. The page of pros-and-cons replaces the action it was supposed to enable. At some point you have to pick the small first step and take it.
reflectionQuestion: What have you been thinking about that you could take one small action on this week?
